channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Adani Enterprises Posts Q4 Loss Despite Revenue Surge, Plans Rs 15,000 Cr Equity Raise
item.description: Adani Enterprises Ltd. reported a net loss of Rs 221 crore for the fourth quarter of FY26. This comes despite a 20.3% rise in revenue to Rs 32,439 crore. Full-year EBITDA held steady at Rs 16,464 crore, thanks to its infrastructure businesses. However, the company's profit was hit by higher depreciation costs from new assets. To support its large infrastructure projects, the board approved raising up to Rs 15,000 crore through equity. A dividend of Rs 1.30 per share was also proposed.

item.title: Oil Jumps, Dips Indian Markets; April Rally Ends Strong
item.description: Indian markets tumbled Thursday as rising oil prices and US-Iran tensions took their toll. However, April finished with strong monthly gains for the Sensex and Nifty, their best since December 2023. Broader market indexes, especially mid and small-caps, performed even better, showing strength beyond immediate worries. Still, higher oil prices continue to strain India's economy, affecting inflation, its current account, and the rupee.

item.title: Central Bank India Profit Dips 30%; Core Business Strong
item.description: Central Bank of India reported a 30% drop in net profit for Q4 FY26, falling to ₹724.4 crore from ₹1,033.6 crore a year earlier. This decline was mainly due to a one-time ₹632 crore deferred tax liability and lower treasury income. However, the bank's core business showed strength: net interest income (NII) jumped 17.8% to ₹4,002 crore, indicating solid growth in its lending operations. Asset quality stayed steady, with gross non-performing assets (NPAs) at 2.67%. The bank declared a 6% dividend and plans to raise up to ₹7,000 crore in FY27 to support future growth and meet regulatory requirements.

item.title: India FDI Soars to $88.3B, Pivoting to High-Value Projects
item.description: India's Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) soared to $88.3 billion by February 2026, a substantial increase driven by government outreach and a revamped Invest India agency. The surge is supported by Free Trade Agreements and a strategic shift towards higher-value projects, with European nations leading in actual investment value. Net FDI also rose significantly to $6.2 billion, positioning India as a more attractive destination in a changing global economy.

item.title: US Economy Splits: AI Surges Amid Inflation, Strong Jobs
item.description: U.S. inflation surged in March, with core PCE reaching a 3.2% annual rate, propelled by an 11.6% jump in energy prices. Economic growth expanded at a 2.0% pace, below forecasts, yet the labor market showed exceptional strength with jobless claims hitting multi-decade lows. This divergence highlights a bifurcated economic reality where technology sectors thrive amidst consumer spending struggles, complicating Federal Reserve policy.

item.title: Eveready Profit Surges 13x With ₹102 Cr Exceptional Gain
item.description: Eveready Industries India Ltd. posted a consolidated net profit of ₹141.8 crore for the fourth quarter of FY26, a substantial increase driven by an exceptional gain of ₹102.7 crore. Revenue climbed 9.4% to ₹327.2 crore, supported by strong performance in alkaline batteries and the lighting segment. The company also commissioned its new ₹200 crore alkaline battery plant in Jammu, aiming to capture premium market share and export opportunities.

item.title: RailTel Q4 Profit Soars 25%, But Shares Slide on Analyst Sell Calls
item.description: RailTel Corporation of India posted a robust 25% year-on-year increase in net profit to ₹142 crore for its fourth quarter, driven by a 28% revenue surge to ₹1,669 crore. The company also announced a recommended final dividend of ₹1.25 per share. Despite these positive financial results and the securing of several new domestic contracts, shares closed marginally lower. Analyst sentiment remains largely bearish, with a "Strong Sell" consensus and price targets suggesting significant downside potential.

item.title: India Gas Shift Falters: Low Compliance Risks Energy Security
item.description: Despite a government order for households to consolidate cooking gas connections and give up LPG lines for Piped Natural Gas (PNG), compliance is very low. Only about 43,000 LPG connections have been surrendered, far below expectations. This lack of adherence poses challenges for enforcement and leaves India vulnerable to LPG import disruptions, especially with ongoing Middle East tensions. The government is reviewing households with dual connections to identify non-compliant users, hinting at potential future actions.

item.title: Quick Commerce Boosts Chocolate Impulse Buys, Straining Premium Brands
item.description: Quick commerce is reshaping the chocolate market, boosting impulse buys and late-night snacking. This shift to frequent, on-demand buying creates a difficult balance: new sales channels may clash with premium pricing and brand goals. Brands must meet rapid delivery needs while maintaining high-quality appeal, especially with fluctuating cocoa prices and evolving value perceptions.

item.title: SEBI Clears Pharma and Steel IPOs Despite Cautious Market
item.description: Hindustan Laboratories and RK Steel Manufacturing Company have secured Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) approval for their Initial Public Offerings (IPOs), with approvals granted on April 27, 2026. Hindustan Laboratories, a B2G pharmaceutical manufacturer, plans a fresh issue combined with a promoter offer for sale, while RK Steel, a steel tubes producer, is launching an entirely fresh issue focused on debt reduction. Both are set to list on the BSE and NSE. This development occurs against a backdrop of a cautious IPO market in April 2026, where secondary markets have shown resilience but investor sentiment for new listings remains subdued due to global uncertainties and past underperformance of IPOs.

item.title: India Auto Sales FY27: PVs and Bikes Surge, Trucks and Tractors Slow
item.description: India's auto sector started fiscal year 2027 with mixed results. Two-wheeler and passenger vehicle (PV) sales surged, but commercial vehicles (CVs) and tractors saw a slowdown. Hero MotoCorp and Bajaj Auto led two-wheeler growth, boosted by last year's low base and better rural demand. Mahindra & Mahindra and Maruti Suzuki drove PV sales higher with SUVs and premium models. However, Ashok Leyland and VE Commercial Vehicles reported sharp drops in CV sales from the previous month, signaling uncertainty after tax adjustments. Tractor sales from Escorts Kubota and Mahindra & Mahindra also cooled seasonally. This split performance highlights differing economic trends, with consumer spending strong in some areas but caution evident in others.

item.title: Godrej Agrovet's Profit Soars 48%, But Margins Compress
item.description: Godrej Agrovet announced a final dividend of ₹11 per share, accompanying financial results for Q4 FY25-26. While consolidated net profit surged 48% to ₹105 crore on a 9.3% revenue increase to ₹2,333 crore, the company's operational health showed signs of strain. Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortisation (EBITDA) declined 5.3% to ₹139 crore, with EBITDA margins compressing to 5.95% from 6.87% year-on-year. The stock closed modestly higher amidst a declining broader market.

item.title: India Industry Credit Surges 15% in March, RBI Data Shows
item.description: Bank lending to Indian industry surged 15% year-on-year in March, according to the Reserve Bank of India. Growth was led by strong loans to large corporations and MSMEs, with the services and personal loans sectors also posting significant gains.

item.title: Mutual Funds Slash Exit Loads, Boosting Investor Flexibility and Rivalry
item.description: Mutual fund companies are significantly reducing exit loads, shortening the holding period from one year to 30-90 days or eliminating them. This move aims to boost competitiveness against passive funds, provide investors with greater liquidity, and simplify fees amid intense market rivalry.

item.title: India Gold Imports Hit Near 30-Year Low on Tax Demand
item.description: India's gold imports plunged to about 15 metric tons in April, a nearly 30-year low. The sharp decline occurred because customs authorities demanded a 3% tax on gold shipments. Banks halted imports due to this new tax and delays in official paperwork, hurting sales during major gold-buying festivals and straining India's trade balance and currency.

item.title: ACC Posts Record Sales But Profits Dive; Merger with Ambuja Looms
item.description: ACC Ltd reported a 68.3% drop in Q4 net profit to ₹238.3 crore, even as revenue grew 18% and sales volume reached a record high. Higher costs for fuel, diesel, and packaging, plus supply chain issues, cut EBITDA margins to 8.5% from 13%. ACC is seeking regulatory approval for its merger with Ambuja Cements to gain scale and efficiency.

item.title: NCC Ltd Bags ₹1,703 Cr Orders Amidst NHAI Legal Battle
item.description: Infrastructure firm NCC Ltd reported ₹1,703.27 crore in new orders for April, primarily from its buildings and electrical segments. This operational success comes as the company navigates a two-year debarment dispute with the NHAI. A Telangana High Court order has temporarily suspended the debarment, allowing NCC to continue bidding. However, the market responded cautiously, with NCC shares closing down 0.39% on April 30, 2026.

item.title: XRP Sentiment Booms on Rakuten Deal, Price Stalls: Contrarian Signal?
item.description: XRP's social sentiment has hit a two-year high, thanks to a Rakuten integration letting users convert loyalty points to crypto. However, XRP's price has barely moved and is down this week. This gap between high optimism and flat prices often signals a contrarian indicator, suggesting current excitement might precede a price drop or pause.

item.title: India Used Car Market Set to Double to $70B by 2031
item.description: India's used car market is set for rapid growth, expected to double to $70 billion by 2031 and become the third-largest globally. This surge is fueled by increasing affordability, shorter vehicle replacement cycles, and a shift where 65% of buyers are first-time car owners. While organized platforms are gaining ground, the vast majority of transactions (80%) remain in the unorganized sector. This, along with a low ratio of used-to-new cars, signals both significant opportunities and challenges ahead.

item.title: Value 360 IPO Launches May 4 to Fund Tech & Influencer Push
item.description: Value 360 Communications is launching an INR 41.69 crore SME IPO on May 4th. The integrated marketing and PR firm plans to use the funds to upgrade its technology, invest in influencer marketing, and pay down debt. This IPO occurs as the SME IPO market faces tougher regulations and changing investor attitudes, requiring companies to show clear growth plans and solid finances. The company's valuation seems competitive in India's growing advertising and PR industry.

item.title: Mphasis Q4 Results Boost Stock: Nuvama Eyes 42% Upside
item.description: Mphasis delivered strong Q4 FY26 results, with revenue up 14.36% to ₹4,242.67 crore and net profit up 14.14% to ₹509.64 crore. Significant AI deal wins fueled this performance, leading Nuvama to reiterate a 'Buy' rating and a ₹3,200 target price, suggesting 42% upside. While its BFSI segment grows well, Mphasis trades at a premium P/E of ~23x and faces broader IT industry AI-driven pressures that could impact future growth.

item.title: Supreme Court Presses India on Airline Fares Amid Financial Woes
item.description: India's Supreme Court is pushing the central government to act on a petition for airline fare and extra charge regulations, expressing frustration over delays. This comes as the country's airlines face severe financial challenges, including rising fuel costs and a weakening rupee, leading to forecasts of large losses. The court's involvement raises questions about passenger fairness and potential changes to how airlines earn revenue from services beyond ticket prices.

item.title: Star Health: Strong Results Clash with High Valuation
item.description: Star Health and Allied Insurance Company Ltd. delivered strong Q4 FY26 results, with net earned premium rising 14% year-over-year to INR 43.3 billion and underwriting metrics improving. Motilal Oswal Securities maintained its BUY rating and INR 640 target price, expecting 32% CAGR in IFRS PAT through FY28. However, the company's current Price-to-Earnings ratio of 56-70x, far above industry averages and its own history, raises questions about its high valuation. Star Health must balance sector growth with justifying its premium price in a competitive market.

item.title: UltraTech Cement's Premium Valuation Faces Test on Growth Targets
item.description: UltraTech Cement's 'BUY' rating and ₹13,800 price target from Motilal Oswal depend on achieving ambitious EBITDA/tonne and ROCE goals. While the company leads in market share and targets strong demand growth, potential margin pressure from rising input costs and a high valuation compared to peers require close attention. Meeting its operational targets is crucial for investor confidence.
